formulas.name: Differentiability Theorem
formulas.formula: If f(x) is a polynomial or a rational function such that all the rational functions have no common factors in their denominators, then f(x) is differentiable.

formulas.name: Cauchy's Generalized Mean Value Theorem
formulas.formula: If f(x) and g(x) are continuous on [a, b] and differentiable on (a, b), then there exists a point c in (a, b) such that (f(b) - f(a)) / (g(b) - g(a)) = (f'(c)) / (g'(c)).
formulas.notes: [for all f and g that satisfy the conditions]

formulas.name: Lagrange's Mean Value Theorem
formulas.formula: If f(x) is continuous on [a, b] and differentiable on (a, b), then there exists a point c in (a, b) such that f'(c) = (f(b) - f(a)) / (b - a).
formulas.notes: [for all f that satisfy the conditions]

formulas.name: Differentiability of Composite Functions
formulas.formula: If f(x) is differentiable at x = a and g(x) is differentiable at f(a), then (g ∘ f)(x) is differentiable at x = a.

formulas.name: Differentiability of Inverse Functions
formulas.formula: If f(x) is a one-to-one function and is differentiable at x = a, then the inverse function f^(-1)(x) is differentiable at f(a).

formulas.name: Chain Rule
formulas.formula: If f(x) and g(x) are differentiable at x = a, then the derivative of the composite function (g ∘ f)(x) at x = a is given by (g ∘ f)'(a) = g'(f(a)) * f'(a).
